channel.title: Cadence 步频 — PT Research Signal
channel.description: Daily curated physical therapy & rehab research for clinicians. AI-scored signal from 50+ journals.

item.title: Critically appraised papers: Adding physiotherapy or motivational text messages does not improve pain beyond home exercise alone for degenerative meniscal tear and knee pain [synopsis].
item.description: 一篇批判性评估论文综述指出，对于退行性半月板撕裂和膝关节疼痛患者，在居家运动基础上增加物理治疗或励志短信并不能比单纯居家运动带来更多的疼痛改善。
item.author: PubMed

item.title: The Psychometric Properties of the Trunk Impairment Scale Used After Stroke: A Systematic Review Using COSMIN Methodology.
item.description: 一项使用 COSMIN 方法学进行的系统综述评估了躯干功能障碍量表 (TIS) 在卒中患者中的心理测量属性，确定了TIS在卒中康复中作为评估工具的证据质量和确定性。
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Two-Year Outcomes of the Proximal Hamstring Avulsion Rehabilitation Regimes: Longitudinal Versus Accelerated Protocol (PHARRLAP) Study: A Randomized Controlled Trial.
item.description: 一项针对60名接受近端腘绳肌腱撕脱修复术患者的随机对照试验（n=30 CR组，n=30 AR组）显示，加速康复方案与保守康复方案在2年随访中，临床结局、力量恢复、功能对称性以及并发症/再损伤率方面无显著差异。
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Critically appraised paper: Mobile-based pulmonary rehabilitation improved exercise capacity as much as and health status more than centre-based pulmonary rehabilitation for chronic obstructive pulmonary disease [commentary].
item.description: 一篇批判性评估论文的评论指出，对于慢性阻塞性肺疾病（COPD）患者，基于移动设备的肺康复在改善运动能力方面与中心化肺康复效果相当，且在改善健康状况方面表现更佳。
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effects of electrical stimulation on lower limb function after anterior cruciate ligament reconstruction: a systematic review and network meta-analysis | BMC Musculoskeletal Disorders | Springer Nature Link
item.description: 一项系统综述和网络 Meta 分析比较了不同电刺激模式结合康复训练对 ACL 重建术后患者下肢功能改善的治疗效果。
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Six-session non-operative exercise program yields sustained benefits for up to 18 months in end-stage knee osteoarthritis: a retrospective cohort study - Clinical Rheumatology
item.description: 一项回顾性队列研究显示，针对等待全膝关节置换的终末期膝关节骨关节炎患者，为期六次的非手术运动方案可带来长达18个月的持续益处。
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Tale of quadriceps and hamstring muscle strength after ACL ...
item.description: 一项带有纵向和多变量 Meta 分析的系统综述，详细探讨了 ACL 重建术后股四头肌和腘绳肌力量的变化。
item.author: BJSM

item.title: Sarcopenia Increases Adjacent Segment Degeneration Risk within 3 Years of Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion.
item.description: 一项对333名接受颈椎前路椎间盘切除融合术（ACDF）的成年患者进行的回顾性队列研究发现，术前肌少症显著增加了3年内发生放射学邻近节段退变（ASD）的风险。
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Effectiveness of physical therapy techniques and methods in the management of endometriosis symptoms: A systematic review with meta-analysis.
item.description: 一项系统综述和Meta分析评估了物理治疗方法和技术在改善子宫内膜异位症症状方面的有效性，发现现有证据的确定性被评为低到极低，主要关注疼痛缓解。
item.author: PubMed

item.title: [Correspondence] Make rehabilitation count: invest in people, not just devices
item.description: The WHO global rehabilitation indicators represent a vital shift from counting disease to measuring functioning and lives lived with quality and dignity,1 and to ensuring rehabilitation is an essentia
item.author: The Lancet

item.title: Management and implementation of intermittent catheterisation in neurogenic lower urinary tract dysfunction: a clinical practice guideline in Germany – update 3.0 | International Urology and Nephrology | Springer Nature Link
item.description: 德国更新了针对神经源性下尿路功能障碍患者间歇性导尿的管理和实施临床实践指南（3.0版）。
item.author: Sports Medicine

item.title: Fusogenic therapy restored spinal cord function after complete transection - Spinal Surgery News
item.description: 一个国际研究团队通过融合疗法和综合康复，在动物模型中成功恢复了脊髓完全横断后的运动、感觉和盆腔功能，该研究成果已发表在《PLOS One》杂志。
item.author: Spinal Surgery News

item.title: Understanding Adult Hip Dysplasia: Clinical Presentation, Assessment Techniques, and Exercise-Driven Management Strategies
item.description: This paper offers an understanding of adult hip dysplasia, covering clinical presentation, assessment techniques, and exercise-driven management strategies.
item.author: JOSPT

item.title: Efficacy and Modulating Factors of Repetitive Transcranial Magnetic Stimulation on Motor Function in Parkinson's Disease: A Meta-Analysis.
item.description: This meta-analysis, including 25 RCTs, evaluated repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS) for Parkinson's disease motor function, finding short-term therapeutic effects but highlighting the need for more research on long-term outcomes and optimal parameters.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Read more about Calendar Year 2027 Hospital Outpatient Prospective Payment System (OPPS) and Ambulatory Surgical Center (ASC) Proposed Rule (CMS-1850-P)
item.description: The CMS has issued its proposed rule for the Calendar Year 2027 Hospital Outpatient Prospective Payment System (OPPS) and Ambulatory Surgical Center (ASC), which will dictate payment policies for these settings.
item.author: CMS

item.title: Establishing weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury: a systematic review and methodological quality analysis.
item.description: A systematic review identified weighted criteria for return to play after hamstring injury, highlighting a lack of clear consensus despite high recurrence rates.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Shortwave diathermy for managing knee osteoarthritis: A systematic review with meta-analysis.
item.description: A systematic review with meta-analysis of 17 trials (n=1,372) found that shortwave diathermy significantly improved pain (mean -2.13; 95% CI [-2.64, -1.62]; p < .01) and walking ability (mean -0.72; 95% CI [not reported]) in knee osteoarthritis.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Read more about CMS Proposes Updates to Strengthen Medicare Program Integrity, Combat Fraud, and Expand Access to Home Health Care
item.description: CMS proposes updates to strengthen Medicare program integrity, combat fraud, and expand access to home health care in the US.
item.author: CMS

item.title: Relationship Between Sarcopenia and Intensity-Specific Physical Activity in Older Adults Living in a Rural Area: An Isotemporal Substitution Analysis from the NEIGE Study.
item.description: This study found that in community-dwelling older adults (n=527), replacing sedentary behavior with moderate-to-vigorous physical activity was associated with a lower risk of sarcopenia, using an isotemporal substitution model.
item.author: PubMed

item.title: Predictive socio-cultural factors of pain intensity, disability, and quality of life in patients with nonspecific musculoskeletal pain: a prospective cohort study.
item.description: 一项前瞻性队列研究发现，在荷兰初级保健物理治疗患者中，社会文化因素（如教育水平、移民背景）能预测非特异性肌肉骨骼疼痛的短期和长期结局，基线以及3、6、12个月的疼痛强度、残疾和健康相关生活质量均与这些因素相关（n=1350）。
item.author: PubMed
